title: RustDesk Server
description: Self-hosted RustDesk relay/rendezvous server (hbbs + hbbr) for running your own private, open-source remote desktop infrastructure.
installation: The rustdesk-server-s6 image runs both hbbs (ID/rendezvous) and hbbr (relay) in one container via s6-overlay. On first start it generates an ECDSA key pair in /data (id_ed25519 / id_ed25519.pub); copy the public key from /data/id_ed25519.pub and distribute it to RustDesk clients as the Key, with this host as the ID/Relay server. Set RELAY to this app's public host so clients can reach the relay. Native RustDesk clients connect over TCP/UDP 21115-21119; those are NOT proxied by the HTTP ingress and require LoadBalancer/NodePort exposure of those ports. Container port 21118 is the hbbs web/websocket port. ENCRYPTED_ONLY=1 rejects unencrypted clients. Persist /data to keep the key pair and the SQLite db_v2.sqlite3 across restarts.
